name: Ibio Climb Loop
description: This 13.7-mile (22.0 km) moderate mountain biking loop, Ibio – circular desde Riocorvo, offers 2471 feet (753 metres) of climbing near Rioco
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cartes offers a diverse landscape for riders. The region features a mix of coastal paths, inland climbs, and routes with views of local peaks. Terrain varies from loops near the sea to more challenging ascents through hilly areas. The network of trails provides options for different skill levels, traversing varied natural environments. Best mountain bike trails around Cartes The most popular mountain bike trail is Suances to Santillana del Mar Coastal Loop, a 18.6 miles (30.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate ride along the coast. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ibio Climb Loop, a moderate 13.7 miles (22.0 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain, providing a good workout and potential views. Local mountain bikers also love the Campa de Ucieda – View of Pico Tordias loop from Viérnoles, a 44.7 miles (72.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of Pico Tordias, often completed in about 5 hours 40 minutes. Mountain biking around Cartes is defined by coastal paths, challenging climbs, and routes offering views of local peaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal loops to more difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Cartes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 2700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cartes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cartes?
A: There are over 150 mountain bike trails around Cartes, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Cartes?
A: Yes, Cartes offers several easy mountain bike trails. There are 8 routes specifically rated as easy, perfect for those new to mountain biking or looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cartes?
A: The region features a diverse landscape, including coastal paths, inland climbs, and routes with views of local peaks. You'll find a mix of terrain from loops near the sea to more challenging ascents through hilly areas.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 44 difficult mountain bike trails around Cartes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14992882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campa de Ucieda – View of Pico Tordias loop from Viérnoles&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 44.7 miles with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that are loop routes?
A: Yes, many mountain bike trails in Cartes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000936777/ibio-climb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibio Climb Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.7-mile circular path that provides a good workout with notable elevation.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike trails with good viewpoints?
A: Many trails offer scenic views, especially those that climb to higher elevations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14992882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campa de Ucieda – View of Pico Tordias loop from Viérnoles&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent views of Pico Tordias. Additionally, you might encounter viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3597279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Montaña&lt;/a&gt; along some routes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cartes?
A: The mountain bike routes in Cartes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain and the options available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or landmarks along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area around Cartes features several natural attractions. Depending on your chosen route, you might pass by beautiful coastal spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Justa Beach&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3688018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Sable Beach&lt;/a&gt;. There are also mountain peaks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4370882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Pico La Capía (Pico Dobra)&lt;/a&gt; that offer impressive views.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in Cartes?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For instance, a moderate coastal ride like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000953840/suances-to-santillana-del-mar-coastal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suances to Santillana del Mar Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes about 2 hours 23 minutes, while more challenging routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14992882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campa de Ucieda – View of Pico Tordias loop from Viérnoles&lt;/a&gt; can take around 5 hours 40 minutes.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by lakes or water features?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate water features. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38181603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de San Cipriano – The Lake loop from Altamira&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 25-mile trail that includes a lake, offering a refreshing element to your ride.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Cartes?
A: While mountain biking is possible year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be warm, especially on exposed trails, and winters might bring cooler, wetter conditions, particularly at higher elevations.
tips: It is recommended to know all the roads that this mountain has, since it has sites of pure mountain biking

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike the Ibio – circular desde Riocorvo route?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is estimated to take around 2.5 hours to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace, fitness level, and how often you pause to enjoy the scenic views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is varied and can be demanding. You'll primarily be on tracks (siempre por pista), but expect sections with a significant amount of loose stones, potentially slippery mud, and steep inclines. Some parts may require advanced riding skills or even pushing your bike.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: A common starting point for ascents to Monte Ibio, which this route circles, is near the Alto de San Cipriano or La Moranca. These locations are found at kilometer 5 of the regional road connecting Riocorvo and Villanueva de la Peña. Parking would typically be available in these areas.

faqs.question: What kind of views and landmarks can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers remarkable scenic beauty. From the summit of Pico del Palo de Ibio, you can enjoy extensive panoramic views stretching from the Picos de Europa to the Basque mountains, and encompassing areas like Castro Valnera, Campoo, Peña Sagra, Cabarga, Cerredo, and the sea on clear days. The trail also passes through picturesque meadows.

faqs.question: Is the Ibio – circular desde Riocorvo trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given the significant amount of loose stones, potential for slippery mud, and steep inclines, this route is generally considered to be of moderate to high difficulty. It requires good physical condition and advanced riding skills, making it less suitable for beginners.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will ride along parts of the Camino del Besaya: Cantabria, the Vía Verde del Besaya, the Subida a Ibio, San Cipriano-Mercadal, and the Pista a Yermo.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal advice isn't detailed, the best time for mountain biking in this region generally avoids the wettest parts of winter and early spring when mud can be excessive. Clear days are ideal for enjoying the extensive panoramic views. Autumn can offer beautiful colors, but be prepared for potentially slippery conditions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Ibio – circular desde Riocorvo trail?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog policies for this exact route is not available. However, as a general rule for trails in natural areas, dogs are often permitted but should be kept under control, preferably on a leash, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that permits or entrance fees are required to access and ride this mountain biking route. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: What should I be aware of regarding trail conditions?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for varied and potentially challenging conditions. The presence of loose stones and slippery mud, especially after rain, can make sections difficult. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out, as conditions can change rapidly in mountainous areas.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is in a natural setting, Riocorvo and Villanueva de la Peña, which are nearby and serve as potential access points, are likely to have local establishments where you can find refreshments or a meal before or after your ride.
